channel.title: OSINT on anastasiiamorozova
channel.description: Recent content in OSINT on anastasiiamorozova

item.title: When the deadline is yesterday
item.description: An interactive scrollytelling piece built with custom JavaScript injected into a CMS that didn’t support it. I added a moving-camera effect to guide readers through a dense network of names and connections across several countries. Named Innovation of the Year in media by Wirtualne Media in 2026. Read the full story.

item.title: Russian diplomatic facilities serve as SIGINT nests in Europe
item.description: We identified signals intelligence (SIGINT) equipment mounted on rooftops of Russian diplomatic facilities across Europe. Verified through satellite imagery and open-source research, the same setup appeared from Warsaw to Budapest, Stockholm, and Brussels.

item.title: KREMLIN LEAKS: secret files reveal how Putin pre-rigged his reelection
item.description: Leaked Kremlin documents reveal the infrastructure behind Putin’s reelection: a pan-Russian propaganda network, internet censorship machinery, and coordinated information warfare operations. We analyzed the files as part of a cross-border team.

item.title: KREMLIN LEAKS: how Putin turned the Red Cross into a tool of propaganda and war
item.description: Leaked documents show how the Russian Red Cross, legally required to stay neutral, became a vehicle for Kremlin propaganda. We traced how Moscow uses it to extend influence abroad under the cover of the International Red Cross brand.

item.title: ANDROMEDA: Ukrainian puzzle
item.description: We tracked the yacht Andromeda, suspected in the Nord Stream pipeline explosions, by cross-referencing satellite imagery and AIS vessel tracking data. On-ground OSINT reporting led us to a Kyiv businessman whose company had rented the boat.

item.title: RUSSIAN ASSET TRACKER
item.description: A joint OCCRP investigation with 25 newsrooms tracking the assets of Russian oligarchs on Navalny’s Anti-Corruption Foundation list of 35. We covered Poland and Central Europe, cross-referencing property records, corporate filings, and public databases.

item.title: See Minsk and die
item.description: We traced financial flows linking companies in the Lukashenko regime’s orbit to migrant smuggling networks bringing people from the Middle East into Europe. OSINT and on-ground reporting uncovered direct ties between the regime and the traffickers.
